slides.lead: Demonstration deck for this template. All text is sample (dummy) content and exists only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Put the event name here, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared view of the forest.
slides.notes: Team slide. Uses initials in a colored circle instead of photos, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Stand condition, inventory data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Section divider between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.h1: The data is already in the woods.
slides.lead: Satellites, drones and field crews collect more than anyone can read. Value appears only when it is connected.
slides.footnote: // illustration slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: higher yield per hectare after thinning the right stands
saved weekly on manual cruise reports
less wood lost to pests with early detection
bullets: Measure impact, not effort: what matters is the effect on the stand.
Start where you already have data and where the loss hurts most.
A small pilot with one clear metric beats a big plan with none.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a label, supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The model does not wait for the loss, it forecasts it.
bullets: Canopy, moisture and growth from key stands.
What preceded past pest and fire events.
Before damage shows up in the harvest.
Thinning into the window that costs least.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same stand, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: Harvest everything at once. Cheap per log, hard on the soil.
Thin on a fixed schedule. Better, but often too early.
Harvest by actual stand condition. Lowest long-run cost.
Critical stands selectively, the rest on schedule.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of modern forestry.
cards: Satellite and drone imagery of every stand over time.
Live view of canopy health and deviations.
Flags unusual decline earlier than a field crew can.
Turns field data straight into a daily stand summary.
Logs stay traceable from stump to mill, under control.
A team that understands the tools and trusts them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Growth slows, so it pays to finish the basics first.
Tools improve fast and reshape established harvest routines.
Automation starts planning the cut, not just measuring it.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a short caption.

slides.quote: The winner is not the one with the most timber, but the one who reads the forest soonest.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, but easy to forget in the field.
bullets: Every stand has the name of a person who answers for it.
You know in advance how you will judge success.
On critical cuts a human always decides.
slides.notes: A plain bulleted slide with a strong lead word. Text scales automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A pilot with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for the key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the tool and build a daily stand summary live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and call to action.
